deep_dive.title: Open-ended Multi-agent Autocurricula via Visual Inspection of Policies with Multi-modal LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Open-ended curricula in Reinforcement Learning (RL) aim to train generally-capable agents by identifying tasks that facilitate learning increasingly complex skills. A major challenge when designing such curricula is assessing task difficulty relative to the agent's current learning progress. While previous work has explored using scalar task scores or textual summaries of the agent's behavior, here we study a different approach: directly inspecting policy behavior via recorded episode videos. We introduce a simple yet effective instantiation of this approach which leverages a Video Language Model (VLM) to both process these videos and provide curriculum recommendations, which we call Visual Inspection of Policies (VIP). Since videos can naturally contain any number of controllable agents, we empirically study VIP on the StarCraft Multi-Agent Challenge (SMAC). We show that even with a lightweight and openly accessible VLM (VideoLLaMa2-7B), VIP can use policy videos to generate more effective curricula than both its text-only ablation and methods that rely on scalar task scores.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent open-ended autocurricula using visual inspection of policies
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于视觉策略检查的多智能体开放式自动课程学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于视觉策略检查的多智能体开放式自动课程学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于视觉策略检查的多智能体开放式自动课程学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Uses a Video Language Model to directly inspect policy behavior videos for curriculum recommendation in multi-agent RL, enabling open-ended skill acquisition.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对多智能体强化学习中的开放式课程设计挑战，提出基于视觉策略检查(VIP)的方法，利用视频语言模型(VLM)直接观察智能体行为视频来推荐课程，避免了传统标量分数或文本摘要的局限性。该方法在多个任务中促进智能体逐步学习更复杂技能，实现了更有效的自动课程学习。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对多智能体强化学习中的开放式课程设计挑战，提出基于视觉策略检查(VIP)的方法，利用视频语言模型(VLM)直接观察智能体行为视频来推荐课程，避免了传统标量分数或文本摘要的局限性。该方法在多个任务中促进智能体逐步学习更复杂技能，实现了更有效的自动课程学习。

deep_dive.title: Provably Optimal Learning Algorithms for Assistance Games
deep_dive.abstract: This paper studies an online variant of the assistance games framework, where an informed agent and an uninformed agent repeatedly interact over $T$ timesteps to optimize a common reward function. While the informed agent (the human) observes a latent state of the world, the uninformed agent (the assistant) observes only the human's actions. We provide the first provably efficient learning algorithms for repeated assistance games. We introduce the notion of assistance regret: the gap between the cumulative utility of interactions and that of the optimal joint policies in hindsight, which map latent states to action pairs. We present decentralized algorithms for both the human and the assistant that achieve a $(1-1/e)$-approximate assistance regret rate of $\widetilde{O}(T^{3/4})$, with runtime polynomial in the size of the action and state spaces. These algorithms are general; in particular, they accommodate any no-regret algorithm for the assistant. We prove that achieving a regret approximation factor better than $(1-1/e)$ is computationally intractable. Furthermore, we demonstrate how these generic no-regret algorithms can be tailored to a pseudo-decentralized setting -- using a shared random string -- to achieve a rate of $\widetilde{O}(T^{1/2})$, optimal up to logarithmic factors.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: decentralized algorithms for repeated assistance games with provable regret bounds
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 重复辅助博弈的分散式算法及可证明遗憾界
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 重复辅助博弈的分散式算法及可证明遗憾界
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 重复辅助博弈的分散式算法及可证明遗憾界
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Provides first provably efficient decentralized learning algorithms for repeated assistance games, achieving (1-1/e)-approximate assistance regret.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究在线重复辅助博弈，其中知情者（人类）观察到潜在状态，不知情者（助手）仅观察人类动作，双方优化共同奖励。首次提出了可证明高效的分散式学习算法，引入了辅助遗憾概念，并实现了(1-1/e)近似辅助遗憾率。该工作为多智能体系统中人机协作提供了理论最优学习框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文研究在线重复辅助博弈，其中知情者（人类）观察到潜在状态，不知情者（助手）仅观察人类动作，双方优化共同奖励。首次提出了可证明高效的分散式学习算法，引入了辅助遗憾概念，并实现了(1-1/e)近似辅助遗憾率。该工作为多智能体系统中人机协作提供了理论最优学习框架。

quick_skim.title: GaP: A Graph-as-Policy Multi-Agent Self-Learning Harness For Variational Automation Tasks
quick_skim.abstract: For robots to work reliably in commercial and industrial applications, can recent advances in agentic coding systems combine interpretable robot programming with the open-world adaptability of model-free policies? We focus on "Variational Automation" (VA), a class of tasks that have larger variations in object geometry and pose than fixed automation. Model-free policies often struggle to close the reliability gap for VA tasks, which must be executed persistently and reliably in commercial and industrial applications. Motivated by prior work on Task and Motion Planning (TAMP) and the Robot Operating System (ROS), we introduce Graph-as-Policy (GaP), a multi-agent coding harness that generates directed computation graphs with perception, planning, and control nodes from a Modular Open Robot Skill Library (MORSL). GaP then generates an internal simulation environment to rehearse task instances with different graphs in parallel to iteratively refine the graph structure and parameters to improve success rates and throughput. Evaluation with 8 new open VA task benchmarks, 4 in-simulation and 4 in real-world, suggests that GaP can achieve success rates that significantly outperform baselines. Details, code, and data can be found online: https://graph-robots.github.io/gap
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent self-learning harness for generating policies via directed computation graphs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体自学习框架，利用有向计算图生成策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体自学习框架，利用有向计算图生成策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体自学习框架，利用有向计算图生成策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces GaP, a multi-agent coding harness that generates directed computation graphs as policies for variational automation tasks, combining interpretability and adaptability.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对变分自动化任务中的可靠性与适应性挑战，提出Graph-as-Policy (GaP)多智能体自学习框架，通过生成有向计算图实现可解释的机器人编程与模型无关策略的适应性结合，适用于工业应用中物体几何和姿态变化较大的场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对变分自动化任务中的可靠性与适应性挑战，提出Graph-as-Policy (GaP)多智能体自学习框架，通过生成有向计算图实现可解释的机器人编程与模型无关策略的适应性结合，适用于工业应用中物体几何和姿态变化较大的场景。

quick_skim.title: Securing Autonomous Vehicle Systems via Twin-Aware Federated Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Federated reinforcement learning (FRL) is crucial for enabling collaborative learning across multiple agents without sharing raw data, thereby enhancing privacy and scalability in the decision-making process within dynamic vehicular environments. However, poisoning attacks pose a significant threat to the security and reliability of FRL-based systems, particularly in safety-critical autonomous driving, where this vulnerability remains largely unexplored. These attacks can compromise the global control model by subtly injecting malicious system parameters, leading to potential hazards. To counter these challenges, we present \alg (\underline{Sec}ure \underline{A}ggregation with \underline{p}oisoning-\underline{p}revention and historical reinforcement) as a defensive framework aimed at enhancing the robustness of FRL systems designed for safety-critical driving scenarios. \alg strategically integrates digital twins for rehearsal-based learning and leverages historical aggregated model parameters along with a selected central gradient to ensure that only benign data is aggregated, effectively mitigating the influence of malicious agents. Theoretical guarantees are provided for the convergence performance of \alg in the presence of poisoning attacks. We also validate the effectiveness of \alg using developed digital twins that model realistic highway environments to evaluate the control of autonomous vehicles under adversarial conditions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: federated reinforcement learning for multiple autonomous vehicles
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向多自动驾驶车辆的联邦强化学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向多自动驾驶车辆的联邦强化学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向多自动驾驶车辆的联邦强化学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes twin-aware federated RL to secure autonomous vehicle systems against poisoning attacks, enabling decentralized multi-agent collaborative learning with robust aggregation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多车辆协作联邦强化学习中的投毒攻击威胁，本文提出Twin-Aware联邦强化学习框架，利用历史奖励与孪生检测技术识别恶意参数更新。在自动驾驶场景中，该方法有效防止攻击者破坏全局模型，显著提升系统安全性。该研究为分散式多智能体强化学习算法的安全部署提供了关键保障。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多车辆协作联邦强化学习中的投毒攻击威胁，本文提出Twin-Aware联邦强化学习框架，利用历史奖励与孪生检测技术识别恶意参数更新。在自动驾驶场景中，该方法有效防止攻击者破坏全局模型，显著提升系统安全性。该研究为分散式多智能体强化学习算法的安全部署提供了关键保障。
